uk: 🕂 Спомин освячення Патріаршого собору Воскресіння Христового в Києві
Службу свв. мчч. Флора і Лавра співаємо на Повечер'ї.
saints.en: Commemoration of the Consecration of the Patriarchal Cathedral of the Resurrection of Christ in Kyiv. Sts. Martyrs Florus & Laurus

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» – 6 стихир: 3 свята[6] і 3 освячення Собору, Слава: освячення Собору, І нині: свята. Вхід. Прокімен дня і 3 читання: 3 Цар. 8, 22-23, 27-30; Муд. 7, 21-8,4; Прип. 9, 1-11. Стиховня свята, Слава: освячення Собору, І нині: свята. Тропар освячення Собору, Слава, І нині: свята. Відпуст середній з помин. свята, служби освячення Собору і мчч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар освячення Собору (2), Слава, І нині: свята. Катизми чергові і після обох – сідальні освячення Собору, Слава, І нині: свята. Псалом 50. Канон свята і освячення Собору. Катавасія Успення – «Прикрашена». По 3-ій пісні – кондак освячення Собору, також і сідальний, Слава, І нині: свята. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос свята. По 9-ій – світильний освячення Собору, Слава, І нині: свята. На Хвалитних – 6 стихир: 3 свята і 3 освячення Собору, Сл.: освячення, І нині: свята. По Вел. славослов'ї – тропар освячення Собору, Слава, І нині: свята. Відпуст середній з помин. служби свята, освячення Собору і мчч.
Часи: на всіх – тропар освячення собору Воскресіння, також Слава: свята; кондаки – поперемінно: на 1-му і 6-му – освячення, на 3-му і на 9-му – свята.
Літургія: антифони повсякденні. По вході – тропар освячення Собору і свята, Сл.: кондак освячення, І нині: кондак свята. Прокімен і алилуя освячення Собору і свята. Ап. і Єв. – дня і освячення Собору. Ряд.: Ап. – 2 Кор. 187 зач.; 8, 16 – 9, 5. Єв. – Мр. 12 зач.; 3, 13-19. Собору: Ап. – Євр. 307 зач.; 3, 1-4. Єв. – Мт. 67 зач.; 16, 13-18. Замість «Достойно» – приспів і ірмос 9-ї пісні канону свята (Успіння). Причасний освячення Собору: «Господи, возлюбив я красу дому Твого і місце обителі слави Твоєї». (Пс 25:8). Алилуя (3) і свята.

troparion_en.text: Let us praise as is meet, O you faithful, / the most comely, radiant and divinely wise martyrs: / Most blessed Florus and all venerable Laurus, / who proclaimed to all the uncreated Trinity. / Suffering unto bloodshed, / they were adorned with brilliant crowns. / Entreat Christ our God to save our souls.

kontakion_en.text: Today all the world honors in glory the divinely wise Florus and Laurus, / as pious martyrs who suffered for Christ. / Through their prayers may we receive grace and mercy / and be delivered from calamity and temptation, / and from wrath and sorrow on the Day of Judgment.

matched_oca_saint: Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Dormition of the Mother of God

troparion.text: In giving birth you preserved your virginity, / in falling asleep you did not forsake the world, O Theotokos. / You were translated to life, O Mother of Life, / and by your prayers, you deliver our souls from death.

kontakion.text: Neither the tomb, nor death could hold the Theotokos, / who is constant in prayer and our firm hope in her intercessions. / For being the Mother of Life, / she was translated to life by the One who dwelt in her virginal womb.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria

troparion.text: Let us praise as is meet, O you faithful, / the most comely, radiant and divinely wise martyrs: / Most blessed Florus and all venerable Laurus, / who proclaimed to all the uncreated Trinity. / Suffering unto bloodshed, / they were adorned with brilliant crowns. / Entreat Christ our God to save our souls.

kontakion.text: Today all the world honors in glory the divinely wise Florus and Laurus, / as pious martyrs who suffered for Christ. / Through their prayers may we receive grace and mercy / and be delivered from calamity and temptation, / and from wrath and sorrow on the Day of Judgment.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Hermes, Serapion, and Polyaenus of Rome

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
Your Holy Martyrs, O Lord,  /  through their contests have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God;  / for possessing Your strength, they laid low the tyrants,  /  and shattered the powerless boldness of demons.  / By their supplications, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O divine Martyrs, you appeared as bright lamps, /  enlightening all of creation with the radiance of your miracles,  / always entreating Christ God on behalf of us all,  / thereby releasing us from our maladies,  / and ever driving away the most profound darkness of ignorance.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Emilian and with him Martyrs Hilarion, Dionysius, and Hermippus

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father John, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father George, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Macarius the Monk of Pelekete

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Macarius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Repose of Venerable John, Abbot of Rila

troparion.text: Your life was a foundation of repentance / and a model of compunction, / an image of consolation and spiritual perfection, / equal to the angels, O venerable one. / You persevered in prayer, fasting and tears, O Father John; / intercede with Christ God for our souls.

kontakion.text: Imitating the life of the angels and forsaking all earthly things, / you fled to Christ, O venerable one. / Protecting yourself with His commandments, / you were shown to be an unshakable pillar against the attacks of the enemy. / Therefore, we cry to you: “Rejoice, O Father John, most brilliant light!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Sophronius of Saint Anne Skete, Mount Athos

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Sophronius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Arsenius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Christopher, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, / O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, / you defended the Faith, even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyrs entreat Christ God to save our souls.

kontakion.text: As venerable and proven ascetics, as honorable and renowned martyrs,  / and as a models of the desert life, O most exalted fathers, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Christodoulos, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
